# Performance Analysis

## Incident Timeline

| Time (UTC) | Event |
|-----------|-------|
| T+0 min   | Alert fires: error rate exceeded 2% threshold |
| T+3 min   | On-call engineer acknowledges page |
| T+8 min   | Initial investigation begins — suspect recent deploy |
| T+15 min  | Root cause identified: misconfigured connection pool |
| T+18 min  | Rollback initiated |
| T+22 min  | Services stabilize; error rate returns to baseline |
| T+30 min  | All-clear declared |

## Root Cause

The connection pool size was inadvertently reduced from 200 to 20 during
a configuration refactor. Under peak load, the pool saturated within
seconds, causing request timeouts cascading through dependent services.

## Impact

- **Duration:** 22 minutes of elevated errors (10 min full outage)
- **Affected users:** ~15% of traffic experienced timeouts
- **Data loss:** None
- **Revenue impact:** ~$20532 estimated

## Action Items

| # | Action | Owner | Due | Priority |
|---|--------|-------|-----|----------|
| 1 | Add connection-pool guard-rail to deploy pipeline | Franklin DuBois | 3 Aug 2026 | P0 |
| 2 | Improve alerting to detect pool saturation before timeouts | Elena Voss | 23 Apr 2026 | P1 |
| 3 | Add chaos-engineering scenario for pool exhaustion | Deepak Singh | 12 Apr 2026 | P2 |
